title: Prompt Security, Untrusted Context, Privacy, and Authorization · Contract Workshop
summary: Harden a tool-using prompt that processes untrusted documents and external results. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a prompt threat model and red-team suite while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate trusted instructions from untrusted data and label authority boundaries.
Recognize direct and indirect prompt injection and prevent authority escalation.
Exclude secrets and irrelevant proprietary context and minimize tool privileges.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
